message:
feat(core): consistent hash ring with virtual nodes for key distribution (wa-9ra8)

Adds a generic consistent hash ring implementation for distributing
keys across nodes with minimal remapping when nodes join/leave.

Design:
- BTreeMap-backed ring of virtual node positions on a 64-bit hash
  space (SipHash 2-4 with node name + virtual index as key).
- Configurable virtual nodes per physical node (default 150) for
  even distribution — higher counts reduce variance at the cost of
  O(V*N) ring entries.
- Binary search lookup: O(log(V*N)) per key resolution.

API surface:
- HashRing::new(virtual_nodes) + add_node/remove_node for membership
- get_node(key) for single-node lookup
- get_nodes(key, count) for N distinct physical replicas (walks ring
  clockwise, skipping duplicate physical nodes)
- node_count(), is_empty(), nodes() for introspection

Use cases in ft:
- Distributing pane monitoring across worker threads
- Load balancing capture work across processing pipelines
- Assigning panes to storage shards

669 lines including comprehensive tests: basic operations, virtual
node distribution evenness, minimal remapping on add/remove, replica
uniqueness, empty ring edge cases, and single-node degenerate case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
